repair_ledger.change_summary: After the initial measurement, if relative_error > 0.04, the skill computes the shortfall (target * relative_error) and adds it to the commanded overaspiration, then re-measures. This compensates for delivery drift so that the effective overaspiration brings the mean volume within 4% of target. A second adjustment iteration provides a safety margin. The water condition (delivery_scale=1.0, relative_error=0.0158 already below 0.04) is unaffected—no re-measurement occurs.
repair_ledger.diagnosis: The glycerol-delivery-drift condition (delivery_scale=0.8) fails volume-accuracy because the fixed nominal overaspiration (6.25% of target = 0.003125 mL commanded) only delivers 0.003125*0.8=0.0025 mL effective, insufficient to bring mean volume within 4% of target. The source documentation explicitly states these are starting calibration values not guarantees, and that measured relative_error should be used to revise overaspiration on subsequent attempts.
